summary: Implemented four fighter-related C functions and one Crazy Hand attribute type correction. The decomp.dev bot reported +464 matched bytes and 3 new 100% matches: `fn_800DC070`, `ft_800C0098`, and `ftCh_Init_80157170`; it also reported `ftKb_SpecialN_800F1420` improved from 0.00% to 95.54%, despite the PR body claiming 100.0 fuzzy match for all functions.

key_files.role: Implemented grabbed-fighter jump escape setup into `ftCo_MS_CaptureJump`.
Implemented Crazy Hand heavy damage/flinch transition into `ftMh_MS_Damage2`.
Changed Crazy Hand attribute field `x24` from `s32` to `float` for motion-coordinate use.
Implemented Kirby copied-hat material diffuse color update over visibility lookup parts.
Implemented fighter cloak/color overlay reset and reapply logic with local optimizer pragmas.

what_changed: `fn_800DC070` now gets the fighter and victim gobj, clears common state with `ftCommon_8007D5D4`, sets escape velocity from `p_ftCommonData->x374/x378`, clears `fp->mv.co.buryjump.x0`, calls `ftCo_800DC920(victim, gobj)`, and changes motion to `ftCo_MS_CaptureJump`.
`ftCh_Init_80157170` now reads `ftCrazyHand_DatAttrs`, computes `fp->mv.ch.unk0.xC.x/y`, changes Crazy Hand to `ftMh_MS_Damage2`, calls `ftAnim_8006EBA4`, and plays SFX `0x4E207`.
`ftCrazyHandAttributes.x24` was corrected from `s32` to `float`, matching its use as the Y component assigned into `fp->mv.ch.unk0.xC.y`.
`ftKb_SpecialN_800F1420` now walks `FtPartsVisLookup` entries, resolves Kirby hat `HSD_DObj`/`HSD_MObj`/`HSD_Material`, and writes the provided packed color into `mat->diffuse`.
`ft_800C0098` now clears `fp->x508` and, when `fp->x2226_b4` is set, reapplies a cloak/color animation using `lb_800144C8` and `Fighter_804D6538` for constant ID `0x80`.
smart_moves: Used a small header fix (`x24` as `float`) instead of masking the Crazy Hand mismatch with casts, improving both type semantics and matching odds.
Scoped `#pragma auto_inline off` and `#pragma global_optimizer off` with push/pop around only `ft_800C0098`, avoiding file-wide optimizer side effects.
Preserved low-level material-color behavior in Kirby by copying a packed `u32` into `HSD_Material.diffuse` rather than expanding it into per-channel assignments.
Kept explicit NULL checks for `dobj`, `mobj`, and `mat` in the Kirby hat traversal, matching the defensive pointer chain visible in the decompiled logic.
Used an assignment expression inside the Crazy Hand `Fighter_ChangeMotionState` argument list for `fp->mv.ch.unk0.xC.z`, a code-shape tactic likely important for matching order.
decomp_lessons: For fighter ext attributes, field type corrections can be the key matching change; verify how fields flow into vectors, motion parameters, or floats before leaving them as `s32`.
When a function must keep constant-looking branches or avoid unwanted simplification, local optimizer pragmas are preferable to broad build-setting changes.
HSD material color updates may match as packed struct writes, so test `*(u32*) &mat->diffuse`-style copies before forcing channel-by-channel code.
Motion-state setup functions often combine velocity/state-field writes, victim/owner bookkeeping, and `Fighter_ChangeMotionState`; preserve call order and temporary use carefully.
Bot match reports and PR-body verification can disagree; archive both, and prefer the decomp.dev byte/match table when recording final match status.
naming_conventions: Unsemantically named functions retained address-style project names: `ftKb_SpecialN_800F1420`, `ftCh_Init_80157170`, `ft_800C0098`, and `fn_800DC070`.
Standard fighter local naming was used: `gobj` for the fighter object and `fp = GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` for the `Fighter*`.
Crazy Hand external attributes were named `attrs` with type `ftCrazyHand_DatAttrs*`.
Kirby model traversal used concise render-object locals: `lookup`, `entry`, `p`, `dobj`, `mobj`, and `mat`.
Ambiguous struct fields remained offset names such as `x5AC`, `x2226_b4`, `x508`, and `mv.ch.unk0.xC`, avoiding unsupported semantic renames.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: `#pragma push`, `#pragma auto_inline off`, `#pragma global_optimizer off`, and `#pragma pop` were used to localize codegen control for `ft_800C0098`.
The constant overlay ID was kept as a local `s32 arg1 = 0x80` followed by an `if (arg1 >= 0x7B)` branch, rather than simplifying directly to the known true path.
Kirby diffuse color uses a packed aliasing write: `*(u32*) &mat->diffuse = *arg1`.
Explicit temporary declarations and NULL branches in the Kirby loop help control generated code shape through nested DObj/MObj/material access.
Crazy Hand's motion-state call embeds `fp->mv.ch.unk0.xC.z = ftCh_Init_804DA070` as an argument, preserving a specific store/evaluation pattern.
review_feedback: No human review comments or review approvals were captured in the slice.
decomp.dev bot reported matched code at 58.75%, +0.01%, +464 bytes.
Bot report: `fn_800DC070` +160 bytes, `ft_800C0098` +156 bytes, and `ftCh_Init_80157170` +148 bytes went from 0.00% to 100.00%.
Bot report: `ftKb_SpecialN_800F1420` improved +141 bytes from 0.00% to 95.54%, which conflicts with the PR body's statement that all four functions had `fuzzy_match_percent: 100.0`.
follow_up_queries: Was `ftKb_SpecialN_800F1420` later fully matched after the bot-reported 95.54% result?
Do later PRs rename or document `fp->x2226_b4`, `x508`, `x488`, and `x408` in fighter color animation state?
Are there additional uses of `ftCrazyHandAttributes.x24` that validate or refine the float type change?
Can callers of `fn_800DC070` and `ftCo_800DC920` clarify names for the grab-break jump and `mv.co.buryjump` fields?
Are there similar HSD material diffuse packed-write functions that can reuse the Kirby `*(u32*) &mat->diffuse` pattern?
